title: Depreciation Methods: A company has equipment that costs $50,000, with a residual...
summary: Calculate annual depreciation expense using the straight-line method given cost, residual value, and useful life.
prompt: A company has equipment that costs $50,000, with a residual value of $5,000 and a useful life of 10 years. What is the annual depreciation expense using the straight-line method?
solution: Using the straight-line method, the annual depreciation expense is calculated as follows: Depreciation Expense = (Cost - Residual Value) / Useful Life. Here, the calculation is: ($50,000 - $5,000) / 10 years = $4,500 per year. Understanding how to compute depreciation expense is critical for accurate financial reporting and asset management. This method is widely used because it provides a consistent expense amount over the life of the asset, simplifying budgeting and forecasting.
